description: The default Amaco workflow: plan → architect → execute → validate → review → fix → verify. Stages are defined in src/workflow/default-workflow.ts; the per-stage prose lives in the docs generator.

stages.simple: Read the task and write a plan.
stages.what: Planner agent reads task + project rules + skills, writes a structured plan that names the files and behaviors it intends to change.

stages.simple: Sketch how the plan fits into the codebase.
stages.what: Architect agent expands the plan with module boundaries, data flow, and interface contracts before any code is written.

stages.simple: Apply the changes in an isolated git worktree.
stages.what: Executor agent edits files inside the run's worktree. Edits are real — never touches the project root.
Runs `commands.validate` from project.yml (typecheck, tests, build, lint). Output is recorded; failures route to fix.

stages.simple: Have a different agent critique the diff.
stages.what: Reviewer agent compares plan to diff, reads validation output, and emits APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / BLOCKED.
Fixer agent ingests review findings + validation logs, patches the diff, then control returns to validating → reviewing.

stages.simple: Confirm the run is ready to merge.
stages.what: Verifier agent does a final pass — checks for unresolved findings, missing tests, or untouched validation gates. Emits PASSED / FAILED / NEEDS_HUMAN.
